reasoning: The target threshold requires more than 10 times the baseline value of 1.75, meaning Liberia would need to experience 18 or more fatalities recorded by ACLED in a single 30-day window. Over the past year leading up to August 2025, Liberia has only experienced around 9 fatalities in total (~0.75 per month). A single month with 18+ fatalities would represent a major unexpected crisis, such as a mass casualty terrorist attack or an outbreak of civil conflict, which is historically rare in post-war Liberia. Recent anti-government protests in mid-July 2025 passed peacefully without reported fatalities. Senator Prince Johnson, a former warlord and potential source of armed resistance to the pending war crimes court, passed away in late 2024, further reducing the risk of organized violent backlash. Due to the lack of any significant violent outbreaks in late July/early August, the probability for the most imminent resolution date is negligible. The likelihood slightly increases for dates further out representing the background risk of an unpredictable crisis, yet remains extremely low due to Liberia's overall stability. (Probabilities are floored at 0.05 per instructions.)
